text.title: go-concurrency-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-concurrency-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Spawning `len(items)` goroutines unconditionally for a slice.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For large slices this creates thousands of goroutines simultaneously, exhausting memory and scheduler capacity. Use a bounded pool instead.

failure-mode.description: `for range jobs { job() }` without a `select` on `ctx.Done()`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The `range` loop blocks until the channel is closed; if the context is cancelled first, the goroutine ignores it and keeps processing.

failure-mode.description: Worker pool with `_ = job(ctx)` discarding errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silently swallows all job errors; callers have no way to detect failures, leading to silent data loss or corruption.
